topic.name: sustainable energy solutions
topic.description: The documents discuss various approaches and technologies aimed at promoting sustainable energy practices and reducing environmental impact.

doc1: impact of solar energy on reducing carbon emissions
doc2: benefits of wind energy in urban areas
strategies for implementing sustainable energy policies

topic.name: Music and Pop Culture References
topic.description: The documents explore various songs and their connections to pop culture, media, and personal narratives.

1: The episode features several songs including 'Car Wash' and 'Y.M.C.A.'
Michael sets up a 'cafe disco' to relieve office tension.
2: The song 'Better than Today' is a pop song emulating various styles.
It peaked on the US Hot Dance Club Songs chart.
3: 'Next to You' features Chris Brown and Justin Bieber, discussing love in a relationship.
The song received positive reviews for its vocal blending.
4: 'Mulder and Scully' references characters from 'The X-Files'.
The song became Catatonia's break-out hit.
5: 'Pittsburgh Town' addresses labor and environmental issues in Pittsburgh.
It reflects historical context and cultural significance.
6: 'Ding Dong, Ding Dong' was written as a New Year’s Eve singalong.
The song incorporates Phil Spector's Wall of Sound technique.
7: 'Tabloid Junkie' critiques media bias and personal rumors about Michael Jackson.
It reflects dissatisfaction with negative media coverage.
8: 'Cat Daddy' is based on a viral dance and became popular on YouTube.
Chris Brown performed the dance on a special event.
9: 'Do You ...' is a love song with playful lyrics and metaphorical references.
It was well received by critics and charted on Billboard.
10: 'We’ll Never Have to Say Goodbye Again' explores themes of love and longing.
The song was covered by various artists and achieved chart success.

topic.name: Naval Warships History
topic.description: The documents discuss various classes of naval warships, their designs, historical significance, and operational history within different naval forces.

Document 1: Kaiser Friedrich III-class battleships were a class of pre – World War I, pre-dreadnought battleships of the German Kaiserliche Marine.
The five Kaiser Friedrich III-class battleships were assigned to the I Squadron of the Home Fleet after commissioning.
Document 2: SMS Falke was an unprotected cruiser of the Bussard class, built for the Imperial German Navy.
She served abroad for the majority of her career, seeing duty in East Asia, the Central Pacific, and the Americas.
Document 3: The Océan class ironclads were a group of three wooden-hulled armored frigates built for the French Navy in the mid to late 1860s.
Océan attempted to blockade Prussian ports in the Baltic Sea in 1870 during the Franco-Prussian War.
Document 4: The Schwalbe class of unprotected cruisers were the first ships of the type built for the German Kaiserliche Marine.
They were armed with a main battery of eight 10.5-centimeter (4.1 in) guns and could steam at a speed of 13.5 knots.
Document 5: Oryol was a Borodino-class battleship built for the Russian Imperial Navy in the first decade of the 20th century.
The ship was completed a few months before the start of the Russo-Japanese War in February 1904.
Document 6: SMS Ariadne was the fifth member of the ten-ship Gazelle class of light cruisers, built by the Imperial German Navy.
She was used to patrol the Heligoland Bight.
Document 7: HMS Perseus was a Colossus-class light fleet aircraft carrier built for the Royal Navy during World War II.
She was converted for use as a ferry carrier to transport aircraft, troops and equipment overseas.
Document 8: HMS Swordfish was an experimental submarine built for the Royal Navy before the First World War.
Swordfish proved to be slower than designed and unstable while surfacing.
Document 9: HMS Marlborough was an Iron Duke-class battleship of the British Royal Navy, named in honour of John Churchill, 1st Duke of Marlborough.
She served with the Grand Fleet for the duration of the war.
Document 10: Libia was a protected cruiser built in Italy in the 1900s.
The ship had originally been laid down in 1907 for the Ottoman Navy.

topic.name: State Highways and Routes
topic.description: The documents discuss various state highways and routes across different regions, detailing their histories, connections, and geographical significance.

Document 1: MD 363 is a state highway in the U.S. state of Maryland.
The state highway runs 18.30 miles from a dead end on Deal Island east to Mansion Avenue in Princess Anne.
Document 4: Maryland Route 285 (MD 285) is a state highway in the U.S. state of Maryland.
The state highway runs 2.43 miles from MD 213 east to the Delaware state line within Chesapeake City.
Document 8: Maryland Route 291 (MD 291) is a state highway in the U.S. state of Maryland.
The state highway runs 18.34 miles from MD 20 in Chestertown east to the Delaware state line.
Document 3: Interstate 705 (I-705) is a short Interstate Highway spur route of Interstate 5 located entirely within Tacoma, Pierce, Washington, United States.
I-705 serves as the connector between Interstate 5, Downtown Tacoma, Tacoma's waterfront, North Tacoma, and the Tacoma Dome.
Document 10: Connector M-13 is a state trunkline highway running 2.41 miles connecting Interstate 75 / US Highway 23 to M-13 near the community of Kawkawlin.
The freeway was once the northern end of the US 23 freeway in the area before I-75 was built.

topic.name: Tropical Cyclone Seasons
topic.description: The documents discuss various tropical cyclone seasons, detailing the formation, impact, and historical significance of numerous hurricanes and tropical storms across different regions.

Document 1: 2003 North Indian Ocean cyclone season was mostly focused in the Bay of Bengal
the storm eventually made landfall in Myanmar on May 19.
Document 2: The 1940 Louisiana hurricane caused record flooding across much of the Southern United States
the storm strengthened further into a Category 2 hurricane.
Document 3: Hurricane Otis was a moderate hurricane that threatened the Baja California Peninsula
the storm degenerated into a tropical depression on October 3.
Document 4: The 1928 Atlantic hurricane season featured the Okeechobee hurricane
the storm then crossed the Bahamas as a Category 4 hurricane.
Document 5: Hurricane Barry was the fourth tropical depression of the inactive 1983 Atlantic hurricane season
the storm made landfall on the northern Mexican coastline later that day.
Document 6: The 2004 Atlantic hurricane season was the costliest Atlantic hurricane season on record
Hurricane Charley made landfall in Florida as a Category 4 hurricane.
Document 7: Tropical Storm Ana was the only tropical cyclone on record in the North Atlantic basin to exist in the month of April
the storm brushed Bermuda with light rain.
Document 8: Tropical Storm Julio was a tropical storm that made landfall on the southern tip of Baja California Sur
the storm brought locally heavy rainfall to southern Baja California.
Document 9: Tropical Storm Gert was the fourth of seven tropical cyclones to make landfall in Mexico during 2005
the storm struck in approximately the same area as Hurricane Emily just four days earlier.
Document 10: The 1939 Atlantic hurricane season officially began on June 12, 1939
the season had below normal activity, with only six tropical storms.

topic.name: Baseball and Cricket Players
topic.description: The documents discuss notable players and events in the sports of baseball and cricket.

1: Jake Diekman is an American professional baseball pitcher with the Texas Rangers of Major League Baseball (MLB).
He previously played for the Philadelphia Phillies.
2: Sourav Ganguly is a former Indian cricketer and captain of the Indian national team.
He is regarded as one of India's most successful captains in modern times.
3: Alan Kippax was a cricketer for New South Wales (NSW) and Australia.
He is regarded as one of the great stylists of Australian cricket.
5: Miller Huggins was an American professional baseball player and manager.
He managed the New York Yankees, winning three World Series championships.
6: Curt Roberts was the first black Major League player for the Pittsburgh Pirates.
He played three seasons for the Pirates in Major League Baseball.
7: Oyster Burns was a professional baseball player whose career spanned 15 seasons.
He predominately played as an outfielder.
9: Iván Rodríguez is a retired Major League Baseball catcher.
He is widely regarded as one of the best defensive catchers of all time.
10: Jos Buttler is an English cricketer who plays for Lancashire County Cricket Club and England.
He is the current Vice-Captain of the England ODI team.

topic.name: Fungi and Mycology
topic.description: The documents discuss various species of fungi, their characteristics, habitats, and taxonomic classifications.

Document 1: Lactarius alnicola , commonly known as the golden milkcap , is a species of fungus in the Russulaceae family.
The fungus is found in the western United States and Mexico, where it grows in mycorrhizal associations with various coniferous trees species.
Document 2: Clavaria fragilis , commonly known as fairy fingers , white worm coral , or white spindles , is a species of fungus in the family Clavariaceae.
The fungus is the type species of the genus Clavaria and is a typical member of the clavarioid or club fungi.
Document 4: Amanita onusta , commonly known as the loaded Lepidella or the gunpowder Lepidella , is a species of fungus in the Amanitaceae family of mushrooms.
The species is distributed in eastern North America , from Nova Scotia to Mexico , and may be found growing on the ground in deciduous forests.
Document 7: Tylopilus tabacinus is a species of bolete fungus in the Boletaceae family.
It is characterized by a tawny-brown cap measuring up to 17.5 cm in diameter, and a reticulated stem up to 16.5 cm long.

topic.name: Medieval Castles and Fortifications
topic.description: The documents discuss various medieval castles and fortifications across England and Venezuela, detailing their histories, architectural features, and significance.

Document 1: Halton Castle is in the former village of Halton which is now part of the town of Runcorn, Cheshire, England.
It was the seat of the Barons of Halton from the 11th century until the 14th century and it then passed to the Duchy of Lancaster.
Document 2: Santa Rosa de la Eminencia castle is a colonial castle built in the seventeenth century by the Spanish monarchy in La Asunción, Venezuela.
The castle served as a prison for war heroine Luisa Cáceres de Arismendi between November 1815 and January 1816.
Document 4: Longtown Castle is a ruined Norman motte-and-bailey fortification in Longtown, Herefordshire.
Built around 1175 by Hugh de Lacy, possibly reusing former Roman earthworks.
Document 5: Bowes Castle was a medieval castle in the village of Bowes in County Durham, England.
Built within the perimeter of the former Roman fort of Lavatrae, the early timber castle on the site was replaced by a more substantial stone structure between 1170 and 1174.
Document 6: Bedford Castle was a large medieval castle in Bedford, England.
Built after 1100 by Henry I, the castle played a prominent part in both the civil war of the Anarchy and the First Barons' War.
Document 8: Brough Castle is a ruined castle in the village of Brough, Cumbria, England.
The castle was built by William Rufus around 1092 within the old Roman fort of Verterae to protect a key route through the Pennine mountains.
Document 9: Berkhamsted Castle is a Norman motte-and-bailey castle in Berkhamsted, Hertfordshire.
The castle was built to obtain control of a key route between London and the Midlands during the Norman conquest of England in the 11th century.
Document 10: Cowes Castle, also known as West Cowes Castle, is a Device Fort in Cowes on the Isle of Wight.
Originally built by Henry VIII in 1539 to protect England against the threat of invasion from France and the Holy Roman Empire.

topic.name: arcade and console video games
topic.description: The documents discuss various video games, focusing on their development, gameplay mechanics, and cultural impact in arcade and console formats.

1: Half-Life 2 : Survivor is an arcade game based on the science fiction first-person shooter video game, Half-Life 2.
The game was met with a generally positive reception.
2: Mario Bros. is a platform game published and developed for arcades by Nintendo in 1983.
The original versions of Mario Bros. were received positively by critics.
3: Donkey Kong Jr. Math is a Nintendo edutainment video game where players must solve math problems in order to win.
It has also received criticism from several publications including IGN.
4: Neverwinter Nights 2 : Mask of the Betrayer is a role-playing video game developed by Obsidian Entertainment.
The expansion received generally favorable reviews upon its release.
5: Ever 17 : The Out of Infinity is a visual novel video game developed by KID.
The gameplay consists of the player reading the game's story, at certain points making choices that affect its direction.
6: Super Mario Bros. is a platform video game developed and published by Nintendo as a pseudo-sequel to the 1983 game, Mario Bros.
The game has sold enormously well, and was the best-selling game of all time for a single platform for approximately three decades.
7: Guitar Hero II is a music rhythm game developed by Harmonix, published by Activision.
Since its release, Guitar Hero II has been met with both critical and commercial success.
8: Star Trek : Voyager – Elite Force is a first-person shooter video game developed by Raven Software.
The game was one of several Star Trek games announced after Activision secured the license to Star Trek video games.
9: Guitar Hero Smash Hits features 48 songs originally featured in five previous games in the series.
The game was highly criticized for being a full-cost standalone title instead of being downloadable content.
10: Shin Megami Tensei : Digital Devil Saga is a duology of role-playing video games developed by Atlus for the PlayStation 2.
The games were published in Europe by Ghostlight and in other regions by Atlus.

topic.name: Naval Battles in History
topic.description: This topic encompasses significant naval battles and military campaigns throughout history, focusing on their strategic implications and outcomes.

Document 1: The Battle of Lade was a naval battle which occurred during the Ionian Revolt, in 494 BC.
It was fought between an alliance of the Ionian cities and the Persian Empire.
Document 2: The Battle of Long Island was the first major battle of the American Revolutionary War.
It was a victory for the British Army and the beginning of a successful campaign that gave them control of New York.
Document 3: Grey's raid was part of British operations in the American Revolutionary War.
The raid was one of the first in a series executed by the British against American coastal communities.
Document 4: The Siege of Fort William Henry was conducted in August 1757 by French General Louis-Joseph de Montcalm against the British-held Fort William Henry.
The fort was garrisoned by a poorly supported force of British regulars and provincial militia.
Document 5: The Battle of the Eurymedon was a double battle, taking place both on water and land, between the Delian League of Athens and the Persian Empire.
It forms part of the Wars of the Delian League, part of the larger Greco-Persian Wars.
Document 6: The Battle of Tellicherry was a naval action fought off the Indian port of Tellicherry between British and French warships.
The engagement occurred during the Third Anglo-Mysore War.
Document 10: The Battle of Solachon was fought in 586 CE between the East Roman forces and the Sassanid Persians.
The engagement was part of the long and inconclusive Byzantine – Sassanid War.

topic.name: Military Engagements in Europe
topic.description: This topic encompasses various military battles and formations in European history, particularly during the Eighty Years' War and the Napoleonic Wars, as well as World War II.

Document 1: The Battle of Zutphen was fought on 22 September 1586, near the village of Warnsveld and the town of Zutphen, the Netherlands, during the Eighty Years' War.
It was fought between forces of the United Provinces of the Netherlands, aided by the English, against the Spanish.
Document 2: The Polish Legions in the Napoleonic period were several Polish military units that served with the French Army, mainly from 1797 to 1803.
They became known as the 'Polish Legions'; a Polish army in exile, under French command.
Document 3: The Arnhem Oosterbeek War Cemetery is home to 1759 graves from the Second World War.
Most of the men buried in the cemetery were Allied servicemen killed in the Battle of Arnhem.
Document 4: The 104th Infantry Division was activated in 1921 and deployed during World War II, seeing almost 200 days of fighting in northwestern Europe.
This division was in central Germany opposite the troops of its allies from the Soviet Army.
Document 5: The Second Battle of Kehl occurred on 18 September 1796, when General Franz Petrasch's Austrian and Imperial troops stormed the French-held bridgehead over the Rhine river.
This battle was part of the Rhine Campaign of 1796, in the French Revolutionary War of the First Coalition.
Document 7: The 14th/32nd Battalion was an infantry battalion of the Australian Army which served during the Second World War.
It was formed in September 1942 by the amalgamation of the 14th and 32nd Battalions.
Document 9: The 4th Parachute Brigade was assigned to the 1st Airborne Division, just prior to the Allied invasion of Sicily.
In September 1944, the brigade formed part of the second day's parachute landings at the Battle of Arnhem.

topic.name: Early American Political Figures
topic.description: This topic encompasses notable political figures in early American history who played significant roles in their respective regions and contributed to the development of state governance and political systems.

Document 1: Thomas Cushing was an American lawyer, merchant, and statesman from Boston, Massachusetts.
He represented Massachusetts in the First and Second Continental Congresses.
Document 2: Jonathan Jennings was the first governor of Indiana and a nine-term congressman from Indiana.
He became involved in a dispute with the territorial governor, leading him to enter politics.

Document 3: General Joel Palmer was an American pioneer of the Oregon Territory.
Palmer served in both branches of the Oregon Legislative Assembly.

topic.name: Professional Wrestling History
topic.description: This topic covers the history and significant events of professional wrestling, including notable wrestlers, championships, and major wrestling promotions.

Document 1: Sherri Martel was an American professional wrestler and manager, better known by her ring names, Sherri Martel and Sensational Sherri.
Martel began her professional wrestling career in the Mid South after training in Columbia, South Carolina.
Document 2: Jos LeDuc was a Canadian professional wrestler better known by his ring name, Jos LeDuc.
Wrestling with a lumberjack gimmick, he debuted in Stampede Wrestling with his kayfabe brother, Paul LeDuc.
Document 3: WrestleMania X was the tenth annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view event produced by the World Wrestling Federation.
The central focus of the pay-per-view was the WWF World Heavyweight Championship, which was defended in two matches.
Document 4: Royal Rumble (1993) was the sixth annual Royal Rumble professional wrestling pay-per-view event produced by the World Wrestling Federation (WWF).
The main event was a Royal Rumble match, a battle royal in which thirty wrestlers competed.
Document 6: The Fabulous Kangaroos was a professional wrestling tag team that existed in various forms from 1957 until 1983.
They are often credited with popularizing tag team wrestling in the late 1950s and 1960s.
Document 8: WrestleMania was the first annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view event produced by the World Wrestling Federation.
The event was seen by over one million viewers through closed-circuit television, making it the largest showing of an event on closed-circuit television in the United States at the time.

topic.description: This topic encompasses various strategies and technologies aimed at promoting renewable energy sources and reducing environmental impact.

doc1: emphasis on solar and wind energy
doc2: analysis of biofuels and their benefits
case studies on sustainable energy practices

topic.name: Indian Bilingual Films
topic.description: The documents discuss notable Indian bilingual films from the early 1950s, focusing on their plots, production details, and cultural themes.

Document 1: Pathala Bhairavi is a 1951 Indian bilingual fantasy film
The film was produced by B. Nagi Reddy and Aluri Chakrapani under the banner Vijaya Vauhini Studios.
Document 2: Pelli Chesi Choodu is a 1952 Indian bilingual satirical comedy film
The film was made simultaneously in Telugu and Tamil with the title Kalyanam Panni Paar.

topic.name: Historical Expansion and Influence
topic.description: The documents discuss the military expansion of the Han dynasty and its cultural influences on newly conquered territories.

Document 1: The southward expansion of the Han dynasty comprises a series of military campaigns and expeditions in what is now modern South China and Northern Vietnam.
Chinese culture influenced the newly conquered territories, and merged with native traditions.

topic.name: Theme Park Attractions
topic.description: The documents discuss various theme park attractions, including roller coasters and dark rides, highlighting their history, development, and unique features.

1: Disaster Transport was an enclosed steel bobsled roller coaster built by Intamin at Cedar Point in Sandusky, Ohio.
It was notable as being the only indoor roller coaster at Cedar Point and the only bobsled roller coaster in the midwest at its debut.
2: Baby One More Time Tour was the debut concert tour by American recording artist Britney Spears.
The tour supported her debut studio album and visited the United States and Canada.
3: Harry Potter and the Forbidden Journey is a motion-based dark ride located in The Wizarding World of Harry Potter themed areas.
The ride takes guests through scenes and environments in and around Hogwarts Castle from the Harry Potter series of books and films.
4: Dominator is a steel floorless roller coaster built by Bolliger & Mabillard at Kings Dominion in Doswell, Virginia.
It is also the world's longest floorless coaster at 4,210 feet.
6: Superman: Escape from Krypton is a steel shuttle roller coaster located at Six Flags Magic Mountain in Valencia, California.
When it opened in 1997, it was the tallest roller coaster in the world.
7: Men in Black: Alien Attack is an interactive dark ride located at Universal Studios Florida in Orlando, Florida.
The ride is themed to the 1997 film, Men in Black (MIB).
9: Apocalypse is a steel stand-up roller coaster located at Six Flags America in Prince George's County, Maryland.
The ride made its debut in 1990 as Iron Wolf at Six Flags Great America before being relocated to Six Flags America.

topic.name: Television Series and Writers
topic.description: This cluster discusses various television series, their episodes, and the writers and producers involved in their creation.

Document 1: A Many Splendored Thing is the second season finale of the American police drama television series Homicide: Life on the Street
the episode featured the second of two guest appearances by actress Julianna Margulies
Document 2: Richard James 'Rich' Appel is an American writer, producer and former attorney
he moved into comedy writing when he was hired for The Simpsons
Document 3: Ghost of a Chance is the second episode of the first season of the American police drama television series Homicide: Life on the Street
the episode marked the first major developments in the Watson case
Document 4: Sexual Healing is the fourteenth season premiere of the American animated television series South Park
the episode parodies several elements of the Woods scandal
Document 5: The Beginning is the first episode of the sixth season of the science fiction television series The X-Files
the episode helps explore the series' overarching mythology
Document 6: Daniel Louis 'Dan' Castellaneta is an American actor, voice actor, comedian, singer and screenwriter
noted for his long-running role as Homer Simpson on the animated television series The Simpsons
Document 7: The Coon is the second episode of the thirteenth season of the American animated television series South Park
the episode parodied several dark-toned comic book films that had been recently released at the time
Document 8: You 're Getting Old is the seventh episode and the mid-season finale of the fifteenth season of the American animated television series South Park
the episode created significant media attention
Document 9: Bill Oakley is an American television writer and producer, known for his work on the animated comedy series The Simpsons
he attempted to include several emotional episodes focusing on the Simpson family
Document 10: Kenny McCormick is a main character in the animated adult television series South Park
he debuted on television when South Park first aired on August 13, 1997

topic.name: Medieval European Monarchs
topic.description: The documents discuss various monarchs from medieval Europe, their reigns, political struggles, and historical significance.

1: Ladislaus I was King of Hungary from 1077 and King of Croatia from 1091.
Ladislaus supported Henry IV's opponents during the Investiture Controversy.
2: Henry II ruled as King of England from 1154 to 1189.
Henry was an energetic and sometimes ruthless ruler, driven by a desire to restore the lands and privileges of his royal grandfather.
3: Ragnall mac Somairle was a significant late twelfth century magnate, seated on the western seaboard of Scotland.
Ragnall styled himself 'King of the Isles, Lord of Argyll and Kintyre.'
4: James I was King of Scotland from 1406.
James was the last of three sons and began his 18-year detention after being captured.
6: Walter de Lacy was a Norman nobleman who came to England after the Norman Conquest of England in 1066.
Walter was given the lordship of Weobley in Herefordshire after the Conquest.
10: Peter de Maulay was one of King John of England's 'evil counsellors'.
Maulay continued to serve the new king after 1216 but ran into difficulties with the regents for the young King Henry III.

topic.name: Digital Marketing Strategies
topic.description: This topic encompasses various approaches and techniques used in digital marketing to promote products and services online.

topic.name: Sports and Historical Figures
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various topics related to sports, notable historical figures, and their contributions or achievements in different fields.

1: Joseph Berchtold was an early senior Nazi Party member and a co-founder of both the Sturmabteilung (SA) and Schutzstaffel (SS).
He served in World War I and remained in the party after it became known as the National Socialist German Workers' Party (Nazi Party; NSDAP).
4: The 2011–12 Australia women's national goalball team, known as the Belles, played in two Paralympic qualifying competitions and the 2012 Summer Paralympics.
Their first competition was the International Blind Sports Federation (IBSA) World Goalball Games.
6: Marina Mohnen is a 4.5 point wheelchair basketball player who played for the German national team that won the European title in 2005, 2007, 2009, and 2011.
She won a silver medal at the 2008 Summer Paralympics in Beijing, and a gold medal at the 2012 Summer Paralympics in London.
9: Teigan Van Roosmalen is an Australian Paralympic S13 swimmer who is legally blind and deaf.
She competed at the 2008 Summer and 2012 Summer Paralympics.

topic.name: Historical Figures and Events
topic.description: This cluster discusses notable historical figures and events, including their contributions and impacts in various contexts.

10: The Experienced English Housekeeper is a cookery book by Elizabeth Raffald, first published in 1769.
The book contains some 900 recipes and is noted for its practicality.

topic.name: Geographical Features and Infrastructure
topic.description: The documents discuss various geographical features, including volcanoes, rivers, and dams, along with their historical and ecological significance.

1: Henry Bell Gilkeson was an American lawyer, politician, school administrator, and banker in West Virginia.
Gilkeson served as the principal of the West Virginia Schools for the Deaf and Blind until 1888.
2: Mount Baker is an active glaciated andesitic stratovolcano in the Cascade Volcanic Arc.
It is also one of the snowiest places in the world.
3: West Branch Fishing Creek is one of the northernmost major tributaries of Fishing Creek in Sullivan County, Pennsylvania.
The creek's watershed has an area of 33.5 square miles, nearly all of which is forested land.
4: The Tabqa Dam is an earth-fill dam on the Euphrates, located 40 kilometers upstream from the city of Ar-Raqqah in Syria.
The dam was constructed between 1968 and 1973 with help from the Soviet Union.
5: Amak Volcano is a basaltic andesite stratovolcano in the Aleutian Islands of Alaska, USA.
It is located on the eponymous island, 31 miles from Frosty Volcano.
6: Mount Tehama is an eroded andesitic stratovolcano in the Cascade Volcanic Arc and the Cascade Range in Northern California.
Tehama started life some 600,000 years ago.
7: The New Waddell Dam is an embankment dam on the Agua Fria River in Maricopa County, Arizona.
The dam creates Lake Pleasant with water from the Agua Fria.
8: Irruputuncu is a volcano in the commune of Pica, Chile, and Bolivia.
The volcano features lava flows, block-and-ash flows, and several lava domes.
9: Toby Creek is a tributary of the Susquehanna River in Luzerne County, Pennsylvania.
The creek's watershed has an area of 36.5 square miles.
10: Harveys Creek is a tributary of the Susquehanna River in Luzerne County, Pennsylvania.
The creek's watershed has an area of 46.3 square miles.

topic.name: Transportation Infrastructure
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of transportation infrastructure, including railways, commuter services, and associated facilities in different regions.

1: Portland City Hall is the headquarters of city government of Portland, Oregon, United States.
City Hall has gone through several renovations.
3: The Jæren Commuter Rail is a commuter train service operated along the westernmost part of the Sørland Line in Jæren, Norway.
The service acts as a commuter rail connecting Stavanger to its suburbs.
4: The Romerike Tunnel is a 14.580-kilometre railway tunnel in Norway between Oslo and Lillestrøm.
The tunnel allows long-distance, regional and Flytoget Airport Express Trains to bypass the old Hoved Line.
6: Trondheim Airport Station is a railway station located within the terminal complex of Trondheim Airport.
The station was opened on 15 November 1994 along with a new terminal at the airport.

topic.name: Horse Breeds and Preservation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various horse breeds, their origins, characteristics, and the conservation efforts to preserve endangered breeds.

1: The Florida Cracker Horse is a breed of horse from Florida in the United States.
Through the efforts of several private families and the Florida government, the breed was saved from extinction.
2: The Heck horse is a horse breed that is claimed to resemble the tarpan, an extinct wild equine.
Heck horses were subsequently exported to the United States, where a breed association was created in the 1960s.
3: The Rocky Mountain Horse is a horse breed developed in the state of Kentucky in the United States.
Today it is used mainly for trail riding and working cattle.
4: The Lipizzan or Lipizzaner is a breed of horse closely associated with the Spanish Riding School of Vienna, Austria.
The breed has been endangered numerous times by warfare sweeping Europe.
8: The Sorraia is a rare breed of horse indigenous to the portion of the Iberian peninsula.
Today, the Sorraia has become the focus of preservation efforts.

topic.name: sustainable agriculture practices
topic.description: The documents discuss various methods and techniques for promoting environmentally friendly and sustainable farming practices.

topic.name: Culinary Practices and Ingredients
topic.description: This cluster discusses various culinary practices, recipes, and the ingredients used in different cuisines.

Document 1: Crab dip is typically prepared from cream cheese and lump crab meat.
It is sometimes served as an appetizer.
Document 2: Pizza cheese encompasses several varieties and types of cheeses designed for use specifically on pizza.
The most popular cheeses used in the preparation of pizza are mozzarella, provolone, cheddar and Parmesan.
Document 3: The Bacon Cookbook includes more than 150 recipes from around the world for everyone's favorite food.
It describes the appeal of bacon and its variations from different locations internationally.
Document 4: The Good Huswifes Jewell includes recipes for medicines as well as food.
It adds herbs to the spices found in Medieval English cooking.
Document 5: English cuisine encompasses the cooking styles, traditions and recipes associated with England.
It has distinctive attributes of its own, but also shares much with wider British cuisine.
Document 6: Naem is a fermented food that has a sour flavor.
It is a popular food in Thai cuisine.
Document 7: Burger King has had a variety of grilled chicken sandwiches in their product portfolio since 1990.
The TenderGrill sandwich was introduced as part of a series of sandwiches designed to expand Burger King's menu.
Document 8: Pink slime is a meat-based product used as a food additive to ground beef.
In the production process, heat and centrifuges remove fat from the meat in beef trimmings.
Document 9: Bacon ice cream is a modern invention created by adding bacon to egg custard and freezing the mixture.
The concept of bacon ice cream originated in a 1973 sketch on the British comedy series The Two Ronnies.
Document 10: Testaroli is a type of pasta or bread in Italian cuisine prepared using water, flour and salt.
It is traditionally cooked on a testo, a flat terra cotta or cast iron cooking surface.

topic.name: Space Exploration and Research
topic.description: The documents collectively discuss various aspects of space exploration, including missions, spacecraft, and scientific observations in outer space.

Document 2: The Apollo program was the third United States human spaceflight program carried out by NASA.

It was launched August 12, 2005, and attained Martian orbit on March 10, 2006.

topic.name: Umayyad Military Campaigns
topic.description: This topic encompasses the military engagements and territorial expansions of the Umayyad Caliphate, particularly in Transoxiana and surrounding regions.

Document 1: The Battle of Baykand was fought in 729 between the Turkic Turgesh khaganate and its Soghdian allies and the Arabs of the Umayyad Caliphate
The Arab army, under the governor of Khurasan Ashras ibn Abdallah al-Sulami, campaigned across the Oxus River to suppress a large-scale rebellion.
Document 4: The Battle of Arsuf was a battle of the Third Crusade in which Richard I of England defeated Saladin
The battle resulted in the coastal area of southern Palestine, including the port of Jaffa, returning to Christian control.
Document 6: The Arab – Khazar wars were a series of conflicts fought between the armies of the Khazar Khaganate and the Umayyad Caliphate
Historians usually distinguish two major periods of conflict, the First (ca. 642 – 652) and Second (ca. 722 – 737) Arab – Khazar Wars.
Document 7: The Battle of the Baggage was fought between the forces of the Umayyad Caliphate and the Turkic Turgesh tribes
The failure of the Umayyad campaign meant the complete collapse of the Arab control in the Upper Oxus valley.
Document 8: The Siege of Kamarja was fought in 729 between the Turgesh khaganate and its Soghdian allies and the Arab Muslims of the Umayyad Caliphate
Its stubborn defence... ended with the negotiated withdrawal of its garrison to Samarkand.
Document 9: Qutayba ibn Muslim was an Arab commander of the Umayyad Caliphate army who became governor of Khurasan and distinguished himself in the conquest of Transoxiana
A capable soldier and administrator, he consolidated Muslim rule in the area and expanded the Caliphate's border to include most of Transoxiana.

topic.name: Public Art and Memorials
topic.description: The documents discuss various public art installations and memorials that commemorate cultural, historical, and artistic significance in different locations.

8: Allow Me, also known as Umbrella Man, is an iconic bronze sculpture located in Pioneer Courthouse Square in Portland, Oregon.

9: John Hines was a British-born Australian soldier of World War I, known for his prowess at collecting 'souvenirs' from German soldiers.

10: Asa Lawrence Lovejoy was an American pioneer and politician in the region that would become Oregon.

an increase in voltage across the device's terminals results in a decrease in electric current through it

the inductances of both the primary and secondary windings are tuned separately with a capacitor across each

the period in the United States known as the Second Red Scare, lasting roughly from 1950 to 1956 and characterized by heightened political repression against supposed communists.

topic.name: Philosophy and Ethics
topic.description: This cluster explores various philosophical concepts, including morality, scientific inquiry, and the relationship between conscience and ethical behavior.

1: Conscience is an aptitude, faculty, intuition or judgment that assists in distinguishing right from wrong.

The extent to which conscience informs moral judgment before an action and whether such moral judgments are or should be based in reason has occasioned debate through much of the history of Western philosophy.
2: Philosophy of science is a branch of philosophy concerned with the foundations, methods, and implications of science.
The central questions of this study concern what qualifies as science, the reliability of scientific theories, and the ultimate purpose of science.
5: John Hadley is an Australian philosopher whose research concerns moral and political philosophy, including animal ethics, environmental ethics and metaethics.
He proposes that wild animals be offered property rights over their territories, and that guardians be appointed to represent their interests in decision-making procedures.
9: The teleological argument is an argument for the existence of God based on perceived evidence of deliberate design in the natural or physical world.
It is historically closely associated with the concept of Natural Theology.
10: The argument from morality is an argument for the existence of God based on moral normativity or moral order.
Arguments from moral normativity observe some aspect of morality and argue that God is the best or only explanation for this.

topic.name: Texas Coastal History and Ecology
topic.description: This topic encompasses the historical and ecological significance of various locations and figures in Texas, particularly focusing on coastal regions and their development.

Document 3: Bellaire is known as the 'City of Homes,' owing to its mostly residential character; however, there are offices along the 610 Loop within the city limits.

topic.name: Cultural and Historical Contexts

2: General Yannis Makriyannis was a Greek merchant, military officer, politician and author, best known today for his Memoirs.

3: The Anti-Hindi imposition agitations of Tamil Nadu were a series of agitations that happened in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu during both pre- and post-Independence periods.

4: James Morris III was a Continental Army officer from Connecticut during the American Revolutionary War.
Morris began and ran an academy that trained both boys and girls together, a rarity at the time.
5: Golos Truda was a Russian-language anarcho-syndicalist newspaper founded by working-class Russian expatriates in New York in 1911.
The newspaper adopted the ideology of anarcho-syndicalism, a fusion of trade unionism and anarchism.
6: Khmer is the language of the Khmer people and the official language of Cambodia.
Khmer has been influenced considerably by Sanskrit and Pali, especially in the royal and religious registers.
7: The Tajik language has been written in three alphabets over the course of its history.
The use of a specific alphabet generally corresponds with stages in history.
8: Anarchism is a political philosophy that advocates self-governed societies based on voluntary institutions.
Anarchism considers the state to be undesirable, unnecessary, and harmful.
9: The Navajo Nation Zoological and Botanical Park is located in Window Rock, Arizona, the capital of the Navajo Nation.
It is notable among zoological facilities in that it labels its exhibits in a Native American Indian language.
10: Acquainted with the Night is a non-fiction book about various aspects of night.
The book consists of 14 chapters, with one chapter dedicated to each hour of the night.

topic.name: Cultural and Historical Impact
topic.description: This cluster discusses individuals and their contributions to culture, society, and history through various mediums such as film, music, and activism.

1: Michael Anthony Monsoor was a United States Navy SEAL who was killed during the Iraq War and posthumously awarded the Medal of Honor.
Monsoor quickly smothered the grenade with his body, absorbing the resulting explosion and saving his comrades.
2: 'Oh Love' is a song by American punk rock band Green Day, released as the lead single from their ninth studio album.
The song made its debut in the number-one position on the US Rock Songs chart.
3: Paper Mario: The Thousand-Year Door is a role-playing video game developed by Intelligent Systems and published by Nintendo for the GameCube.

5: Ed Wood is a 1994 American biographical period comedy-drama film directed and produced by Tim Burton, starring Johnny Depp as cult filmmaker Ed Wood.

6: Vikram is an Indian film actor who predominantly appears in Tamil language films and has won seven Filmfare Awards.

8: Viva el Príncipe is a tribute album to Mexican singer José José, covering twelve songs by him.

9: 'Hot to the Touch' is the first episode of the fourth season of the animated television series Adventure Time.

10: Maria Ulfah Santoso was an Indonesian women's rights activist and politician, the first Indonesian woman to receive a degree in law.
She was a member of the Committee for Preparatory Work for Indonesian Independence and later became the social minister.
